name: Empyrical (Financial Risk Metrics Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); empyrical-reloaded fork by Stefan Jansen

auth.notes: Pure Python analytics library; no auth

performance.notes: NumPy/Pandas-based; vectorized; sub-millisecond for most metrics

agent_readiness.notes: Standalone risk metrics library from Quantopian. import empyrical as ep. ep.sharpe_ratio(returns). ep.sortino_ratio(returns). ep.max_drawdown(returns). ep.calmar_ratio(returns). ep.alpha_beta(returns, factor_returns). ep.annual_return(returns). ep.annual_volatility(returns). ep.value_at_risk(returns, cutoff=0.05). All functions accept pandas Series of daily returns. Used internally by pyfolio. Programmatic access to risk metrics without Matplotlib overhead.

gotchas: All functions assume daily return frequency by default
Period parameter needed for non-daily returns (weekly, monthly)
best_when: Programmatic risk metric computation (Sharpe, Sortino, drawdown)
When pyfolio tearsheets are too heavyweight
Fast vectorized risk calculations in backtest evaluation loops
avoid_when: When full tearsheet visualization needed (use pyfolio)
Real-time risk management (use purpose-built risk systems)
summary: Empyrical provides programmatic financial risk metrics: Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, max drawdown, alpha/beta, VaR. ep.sharpe_ratio(returns), ep.max_drawdown(returns). Accepts pandas Series of daily returns. Fast vectorized NumPy/Pandas implementation. No visualization overhead. Used internally by pyfolio. Ideal for building custom performance reporting or evaluating strategies programmatically.
